shower.thought: # Shower Thoughts
We spend our entire lives learning how to be human, and we only get good at it right before it's too late to use it.
If you remember something from your childhood, you're not actually remembering the event—you're remembering the last time you remembered it.
You've never actually seen your own face, only reflections and photographs of someone you assume is you.
The only time you're truly irreplaceable is the moment you disappear and someone realizes they never asked you the questions they meant to ask.
We call it "falling" in love, but the only thing falling is your ability to think clearly about it.
Every conversation you're not having right now is a conversation that will never happen exactly this way again.
Boredom might be the most honest emotion—it's you realizing that nothing currently matters enough to trick yourself otherwise.
You'll never know if you love someone or if you just love the idea of not being alone.
The things that make you uncomfortable are usually the only things actually changing you.
Most of your opinions are just inherited beliefs you've never questioned because they came from people you trusted.
You're nostalgic for a version of your past that never actually existed the way you remember it.
Silence is the only place where you hear what you actually think instead of what you've been taught to think.
Every goal you achieve immediately becomes evidence that you should have aimed higher.
We're all just one conversation away from being completely different people, but we never have that conversation.
The scariest part of being an adult isn't responsibility—it's realizing nobody knows what they're doing either.
You could be living the best year of your life right now and you wouldn't know it until years later.
Your biggest fear about dying is probably less about death and more about leaving things unsaid.
The moments that define your life usually feel completely ordinary while they're happening.
We spend years building walls to protect ourselves from pain, then more years wondering why we're lonely.

motivational.thought: The master has failed more times than the beginner has even tried. — Stephen McCranie
Your future self is watching you right now through memories you're creating. — Unknown
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
The only person you're destined to become is the one you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Comparison is the thief of joy, but progress is its antidote. — Unknown
You are not behind. You are not lost. You are exactly where you need to be to become who you're meant to be. — Unknown
What lies behind you and what lies before you are tiny matters compared to what lies within you. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
The cost of discipline is always less than the pain of regret. — Unknown
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to give up. — Unknown
Your mistakes don't define you; your response to them does. — Unknown
Become obsessed with the process, not the outcome. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Unknown
Comfort is the enemy of growth, but growth is the antidote to comfort. — Unknown
What you consistently do is far more important than what you occasionally do. — Unknown
You don't rise to the level of your goals; you fall to the level of your systems. — James Clear
The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it. — Chinese Proverb
Your potential is infinite, but your time is finite. Choose wisely. — Unknown
Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will. — Unknown
Progress over perfection, always. — Unknown
The only permission you need is the permission you give yourself. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my girlfriend she was drawing her eyebrows too high.
She looked surprised.

My therapist asked what my biggest fear is.
I said it was losing her hourly rate.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.

They say money can't buy happiness.
But it can buy a jet ski.
You ever seen someone frown on a jet ski?
That's because they drowned.
I got kicked out of the funeral home for asking if they offered a loyalty program.

My doctor said I drink too much coffee.
I told him that's a latte to process.
He said I'm also in denial.
I said no I'm not, I'm in his office.

People keep asking me why I'm not married yet.
I tell them I'm waiting for someone special.
Someone with a really good life insurance policy.

I asked my depressed friend if he wanted to hang out.
He said he was thinking about it literally.

My coworker said I have no ambition.
That really hurt.
Mostly because he's my boss and I need this job.

I told my ex she was the one that got away.
She said "I know, I'm very lucky."
The alimony suggests otherwise.

My dad's been missing for fifteen years.
I'm not worried though.
He's very good at hide and seek.

I went to a self-help seminar on overcoming fear of commitment.
I didn't finish it.

My friend said his life flashed before his eyes during his accident.
I said that must have been quick.

I asked the pharmacist if antidepressants had side effects.
He said some people experience suicidal thoughts.
I said that's just the medication working.

My wife said I never listen to her.
At least I think that's what she said, I wasn't paying attention.

I told my therapist I think I have amnesia.
He asked when it started.
I said I can't remember.
He said that's not how this works.
I said maybe for you.

My friend's been in a coma for three months.
His family says they're "staying positive."
I think they're just avoiding the hospital bills.

I got life insurance as a joke.
My wife says it's not funny anymore.
She says it's "motivation."
